sub_pixel_system.notes: NES beat-em-up; no sub-pixel movement documentation available in sources

special_mechanics.name: Combat System
special_mechanics.description: Martial arts techniques including basic punches, kicks, throws, and elbow strikes. Can grab opponents from behind in cooperative play to allow unimpeded attacks.

special_mechanics.name: Weapon Pickup
special_mechanics.description: Player can knock weapons out of enemies' hands and pick them up. Available weapons include baseball bats, whips, throwing knives, and dynamite. Environmental objects like rocks, oil drums, and boxes can be used.

special_mechanics.name: Fall Hazards
special_mechanics.description: Player loses a life if falling off bottom of screen or into holes, rivers, or beds of spikes.

physics_values.notes: Indicates gravity/fall mechanics exist but not quantified

special_mechanics.name: Stage Progression
special_mechanics.description: Automatic walking transition sequences between stages. Four missions with different stage types: city slum, factory, forest, gang hideout. Fourth stage has interior layout with traps.

tier_notes: Tier 2 - Empirical/Qualitative data. No quantitative movement or physics parameters documented in available sources. Documentation covers game mechanics, story, development history, and hardware specs but not low-level physics values. Movement feel inferred from beat-em-up genre conventions of 1988 arcade/NES games.
